title: Neottia oblata
taxonomy: Neottia oblata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This plant is a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Neottieae. It belongs to the genus Neottia, which comprises a group of terrestrial orchids found in various parts of the world. The species Neottia oblata is native to China and has been documented in some regions. Neottia oblata is an epiphytic orchid, growing on trees and other vegetation to obtain necessary nutrients.

intro: Neottia oblata, also known as the Asian lady's tresses, is a terrestrial orchid species native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, corkscrew-like flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Neottia oblata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a dried extract, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
